cards.name: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.address: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.blurb: Wander the Japanese-style terraced gardens at your own pace — the bamboo grove and koi pond are worth the slow lap around the whole property.
cards.why: Perfect morning temperature for a quiet outdoor walk before the day heats up.

cards.name: Mount Eden Vineyards
cards.address: 22020 Mt Eden Rd, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Taste through their estate Chardonnays and Pinot Noirs with a view of the Santa Cruz Mountains — bring a snack or picnic to enjoy on the hillside.
cards.why: Mount Eden is one of the most respected names in California Burgundy-style wines and the setting delivers.

cards.name: Picchetti Winery
cards.address: 13100 Montebello Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Continue the wine trail at Picchetti, a historic ranch winery with a shaded patio and a relaxed pour-your-own-pace vibe that makes it easy to linger.
cards.why: Picchetti and Mount Eden are both on Montebello Ridge, so this is a natural second stop on the same scenic drive.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Shake off the afternoon with a flat, shaded walk along the creek trail — the path winds past willows and open water and never asks too much of you.
cards.why: A low-key way to transition from wine country to dinner without just sitting in the car.

cards.name: ShirSpa
cards.address: 15868 Cherry Blossom Ln, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Unwind with a massage or spa treatment at this boutique Los Gatos retreat — a perfect way to decompress after a full day of hiking and wine tasting.
cards.why: You've been on your feet all day, so a little pampering right in Los Gatos is a natural way to close things out.

cards.name: Bowlero Valley Fair
cards.address: 2855 Stevens Creek Blvd #1005, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Roll a few games at Bowlero Valley Fair, a lively bowling alley with great lanes, arcade games, and a full bar to keep the evening going.
cards.why: It's a fun, low-key way to cap off a Tuesday night without straying too far from where you already are.

cards.name: Caffino Drive-Thru Espresso
cards.address: 2790 Homestead Rd, Santa Clara, CA 95051, USA
cards.blurb: Quick drive-thru espresso to get the morning started — grab a latte and shake off the 52-degree chill before heading out.
cards.why: Good coffee fast, so you're not dragging when the art show opens.

cards.name: Jonathan Calm - To Wherever, Forever: Archives of Absence & Sites of Passage
cards.blurb: Explore two linked exhibitions examining absence, memory, and migration through art.
cards.why: Free, thought-provoking, and a genuinely good way to spend a cool Tuesday morning.

cards.name: Iraklis Restaurant
cards.address: 2165 El Camino Real, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Mediterranean spot on El Camino with serious value and solid food. Try whatever's fresh off the grill and settle in.
cards.why: It's the kind of place locals go back to repeatedly for a reason.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Free contemporary art museum with a sculpture garden worth wandering even on a gray day. The indoor galleries are strong, and the outdoor sculptures hold up in cooler weather.
cards.why: The Triton is genuinely underrated and this is a great low-key afternoon anchor.

cards.name: Portuguese Tasty Desserts
cards.address: 1085 El Camino Real, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Portuguese bakery with a knockout reputation. Grab a pastel de nata or whatever's left in the case before they close at 4.
cards.why: A 4.8 rating at a bakery is basically a mandate to stop in.

cards.name: Xiang Xiang Noodle
cards.address: 1115 Tasman Dr #2228, Sunnyvale, CA 94089, USA
cards.blurb: Slurp your way through a bowl of hand-pulled noodles in a warm spot after a full afternoon out. The broth is the move here.
cards.why: Exactly what you want on a cool evening after a day of walking around.

cards.name: West Valley College Aquatics Center
cards.address: Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a swim at West Valley College's aquatics center — lap lanes, cool air, and a clean facility to wake up properly before the day kicks in.
cards.why: Cool morning temps make an indoor pool feel like the smartest possible call.

cards.name: ESL Conversation Club at the Cupertino Library
cards.blurb: You practice English speaking and listening while learning about other cultures in conversation.
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: Perrucci Family Vineyard
cards.address: 15050 Kennedy Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Perrucci Family Vineyard for a midday pour in a relaxed, intimate outdoor setting — this is a small family operation with a genuinely warm vibe.
cards.why: A Tuesday afternoon at a low-key vineyard hits different when you're not fighting weekend crowds.

cards.name: Cloud 9 Foot Spa San Jose - Formerly Oceanic
cards.address: 2989 Union Ave, San Jose, CA 95124, USA
cards.blurb: Book a foot spa session at Cloud 9 and let someone work out whatever the morning hike left behind — it's a solid hour of doing absolutely nothing productive and that's the point.
cards.why: After a trail walk and a wine stop, this is exactly the right gear shift.
cards.venue: Cloud 9 Foot Spa San Jose - Formerly Oceanic

cards.name: Heritage Pub and Chop House
cards.address: 15 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Grab dinner at Heritage Pub and Chop House on Santa Cruz Ave — settle into a booth, order something hearty, and take your time with it.
cards.why: It's a solid sit-down spot right in the heart of Los Gatos for a Tuesday night wind-down.

cards.name: Montalvo Arts Center Arboretum
cards.address: Montalvo Arts Center
cards.blurb: Wind down the evening with a stroll through Montalvo's peaceful arboretum trails, surrounded by towering redwoods and sculpted gardens that feel worlds away from the city.
cards.why: A calm, close-to-home outdoor finish keeps the evening easy after dinner in Los Gatos.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Triton Museum of Art, wandering the contemporary galleries and then stepping into the sculpture garden — the outdoor space is beautiful even on a cool morning.
cards.why: Free world-class art in Santa Clara that most people don't even know exists.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the Los Gatos Creek Trail for a relaxed post-museum reset. The path follows the creek through shade trees — flat, easy, and genuinely pretty.
cards.why: Good legs-and-fresh-air break before the afternoon stretch.

cards.name: Gali Vineyards
cards.address: 21 W Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Gali Vineyards opens at 3 PM right on Main Street in Los Gatos. Grab a glass, settle in, and let the afternoon slow down a bit.
cards.why: Perfect spot to decompress and enjoy a pour after being on your feet.

cards.name: Dio Deka
cards.address: 210 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Dio Deka for dinner. It's the best Greek food in Los Gatos — the patio is worth requesting, and you'll want to linger over the menu.
cards.why: A dinner worth dressing up a little for, and the patio makes it feel like a real evening out.

cards.name: GOLF SQUARE SAN JOSE
cards.address: 2590 S Bascom Ave Suite B, San Jose, CA 95124, USA
cards.blurb: Get an early tee time at this indoor golf simulator and warm up the morning with a few swings before the crowds show up.
cards.why: It's a cool morning and hitting a simulator beats standing outside in 52 degrees.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Walk through the full sweep of computing history, from room-sized mainframes to the personal computer revolution — the exhibits are genuinely wild and the artifacts are real.
cards.why: Two hours in here goes fast, and you'll leave knowing something you didn't before.

cards.name: Main Street Cupertino
cards.address: 19401 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll the outdoor plaza at Main Street Cupertino and grab lunch at one of the restaurants lining the strip, with Apple Park sitting right in the backdrop.
cards.why: It's the most pleasant afternoon spot in Cupertino and lunch options here actually cover a lot of ground.

cards.name: J. Lohr Vineyards & Wines
cards.address: 1000 Lenzen Ave, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Settle in for a tasting at J. Lohr's San Jose winery, working through their reds and whites in a relaxed, no-rush setting with knowledgeable pourers.
cards.why: A Wednesday afternoon at a winery with a 4.9 reputation is exactly what midweek is for.

cards.name: Roja Los Altos
cards.address: 242 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the evening at Roja Los Altos with a cocktail or glass of wine and something from their dinner menu to ease into the night.
cards.why: The vibe here matches the pace of a Wednesday evening perfectly.

cards.name: Grant Park
cards.address: 1575 Holt Ave, Los Altos, CA 94024, USA
cards.blurb: Nice spot to get some air.
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: SJSU Student Union Bowling Center
cards.address: 211 S 9th St, San Jose, CA 95192, USA
cards.blurb: End the night with a few frames at the SJSU bowling center, which stays open late and has a laid-back weeknight crowd.
cards.why: Bowling is always a good call when you want to keep the night going without overthinking it.

cards.name: TrioSpa - Massage, Facials & Waxing / Trio Wellness Mgmt
cards.address: 1585 The Alameda, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Start the day with a massage or facial at TrioSpa. It's a legit spa morning — book whatever sounds good and let them take care of you for an hour.
cards.why: Cool morning, nowhere to be yet — this is exactly when a spa visit makes sense.
cards.venue: TrioSpa - Massage, Facials & Waxing / Trio Wellness Mgmt

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Wander through the Triton's galleries and sculpture garden. The contemporary art rotates regularly and the outdoor garden is worth taking your time in.
cards.why: Free admission and genuinely good art — hard to beat that for a Tuesday morning.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square Market and graze your way through lunch. Grab tacos from one vendor, something from another, find a patio seat and make an afternoon of it.
cards.why: One of the best lunch spots in downtown San Jose — the variety means you'll both find something you actually want.

cards.name: Alum Rock Park
cards.address: 15350 Penitencia Creek Rd, San Jose, CA 95127, USA
cards.blurb: Wander the creekside trails and open meadows at Alum Rock Park, a great spot to stretch your legs among eucalyptus groves and natural mineral springs.
cards.why: A relaxed afternoon outdoors is a nice reset between lunch and the evening entertainment.

cards.name: Mother's Day Craft: Cardmaking
cards.address: 290 International Circle San Jose
cards.blurb: Design and create a handmade card celebrating your mother or mother figure with your own personal touch.
cards.why: It's a low-key, creative way to spend the early evening before heading out for the Irish session.

cards.name: Live Irish Seisiún
cards.address: 25 N San Pedro St., San Jose
cards.blurb: Experience traditional Irish music sessions at O Flaherty's Irish Pub.
cards.why: Traditional Irish sessions are rare and genuinely fun — this one is worth the trip downtown.

cards.name: Ridge Vineyards
cards.address: 17100 Montebello Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Drive up Montebello Road and taste through Ridge's lineup on the hilltop terrace. The Zinfandels and Cabernets are the main event, and the views over the valley are worth the winding road up.
cards.why: A weekday morning tasting means a quieter experience and more time with the staff.

cards.name: Main Street Cupertino
cards.address: 19401 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the outdoor plaza, pick a spot from one of the Asian restaurants or grab a seat at Lazy Dog, and take your time with lunch in the open air.
cards.why: It's the most relaxed midday stop in Cupertino and a good reset after wine tasting.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll the lakeside trails, watch the ducks, and decompress between a big lunch and an evening out in Los Gatos — the park is right there when you need a breath of fresh air.
cards.why: It bridges the afternoon gap perfectly and keeps you in the Los Gatos area ahead of dinner at Dio Deka.

cards.name: Dio Deka
cards.address: 210 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Snag a patio table at Dio Deka and start with drinks and a few mezze before dinner proper. The space is beautiful and the food matches it — dress up a little and take your time.
cards.why: It's the best dinner in Los Gatos and a perfect way to end the day on a high note.

cards.name: Trivia Night
cards.address: 25 N San Pedro St., San Jose
cards.blurb: Challenge yourself and friends to a trivia competition at the pub.
cards.why: A Wednesday trivia night is exactly the kind of low-effort, high-fun way to close out the evening.

cards.name: Monday Morning Meditation & Mindfulness
cards.blurb: Sit for a 20-minute guided meditation session, then ask questions and discuss with instructor Manisha.
cards.why: Free, chill, and a solid way to start a Monday with intention.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the creek trail at your own pace. It's shaded and flat, so you can go as far or as short as you want before doubling back.
cards.why: Cool morning temps make this the perfect window for a walk outside.

cards.name: Bowlero Valley Fair
cards.address: 2855 Stevens Creek Blvd #1005, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a lane and knock out a few games. It's a solid afternoon escape when you want to be active without being outdoors.
cards.why: Bowling is criminally underrated for a weekday afternoon with adults.

cards.name: Cooper-Garrod Vineyards at Garrod Farms
cards.address: 22645 Garrod Rd, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Taste through their estate wines on the outdoor patio at Garrod Farms. The setting is relaxed and unpretentious, and the vineyard views don't hurt.
cards.why: This is exactly the kind of low-key winery afternoon that's hard to beat on a mild Monday.

cards.name: GOLF SQUARE SAN JOSE
cards.address: 2590 S Bascom Ave Suite B, San Jose, CA 95124, USA
cards.blurb: Step into a bay of golf simulators and work on your swing with realistic course play and instant shot feedback.
cards.why: A fun, low-key way to burn some time between the vineyard and your evening shopping stop.

cards.name: Mai Do - Fine Stationery & Goods - Santana Row
cards.address: 378 Santana Row #1125, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Browse the fine stationery, pens, and goods at Mai Do on Santana Row. It's the kind of shop where you go in for a card and leave with three things you didn't know you needed.
cards.why: Santana Row in the evening is a great vibe, and Mai Do is one of the more distinctive shops on the strip.
cards.venue: Mai Do - Fine Stationery & Goods - Santana Row

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Computer History Museum — the exhibits on the origins of computing, the internet, and early software are genuinely fascinating even if you've never written a line of code.
cards.why: Cool and overcast is the perfect excuse to go deep on a museum you've probably been meaning to visit forever.

cards.name: Picchetti Winery
cards.address: 13100 Montebello Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Head up to Picchetti Winery for a glass and some air — the outdoor setting is beautiful and the wines are solid, so grab a pour and sit outside if the clouds cooperate.
cards.why: A winery lunch beats a restaurant any Wednesday.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Walk the trails around Shoreline Lake, watch the windsurfers, or just wander the open bay flats and decompress for a couple hours.
cards.why: The fresh air after a winery visit is basically mandatory.

cards.name: Main Street Cupertino
cards.address: 19401 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll the open-air corridor at Main Street Cupertino for a coffee, light bite, or browse through its mix of local shops and casual dining spots.
cards.why: It's a natural wind-down stop right after Shoreline Park and keeps you loosely in the west-side orbit before heading east to dinner.

cards.name: Smoking Pig BBQ
cards.address: 1144 N 4th St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Smoking Pig BBQ — get the brisket plate, add the mac and cheese, and don't overthink it.
cards.why: It's the best BBQ in the South Bay and you've earned a real meal.

cards.name: Chromatic Coffee
cards.address: Chromatic Coffee, San Jose
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a proper locally roasted cup — order whatever the barista recommends and take a minute to wake up before the day kicks in.
cards.why: Good coffee makes everything else better.

cards.name: SJSU Theatre Presents: Kingdom of Cards at Hammer Theater Center
cards.blurb: Watch Kingdom of Cards, Tagore's play adapted and directed by Sukanya Chakrabarti at Hammer Theater.
cards.why: Free theater this good is hard to pass up on a weekday.

cards.name: Bill's Cafe
cards.address: Bill's Cafe, Willow Glen
cards.blurb: Head to Willow Glen and settle in at Bill's Cafe for a late lunch — the eggs benedict and breakfast burrito are the moves, and the place has a warmth that makes you linger.
cards.why: It's a South Bay institution and the food holds up every time.

cards.name: Luxe Day Spa (Massage Spa)
cards.address: 1445 Foxworthy Ave Suite 40, San Jose, CA 95118, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage at Luxe Day Spa and decompress for a couple hours. Let them do the work — you've earned a slow afternoon.
cards.why: Cool, grey Wednesday afternoons were made for this.

cards.name: Dio Deka
cards.address: 210 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Drive down to Los Gatos for dinner at Dio Deka. Dress up a little, order something from the Greek menu, and grab a table on the patio if the evening cooperates.
cards.why: It's the kind of dinner that actually feels like a treat.

cards.name: Chromatic Coffee
cards.address: Chromatic Coffee, San Jose
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a seriously good cup — locally roasted, carefully pulled, the kind of coffee that makes you wonder why you ever went to a chain.
cards.why: Best way to ease into a Wednesday.

cards.name: English Conversation Club
cards.address: 6445 Camden Ave. San Jose
cards.blurb: Practice speaking English with other learners guided by two leaders discussing different topics each week.
cards.why: Free, social, and a little different from the usual morning routine.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll along the lake trail, watch the ducks, and find a bench to unwind before the afternoon picks up.
cards.why: A peaceful midday outdoor break pairs nicely with the coffee and conversation of your morning.

cards.name: Oasis Thai Spa
cards.address: 2203 S Bascom Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the afternoon with a massage at Oasis Thai Spa in Campbell — book whatever length session fits and let the day decompress before dinner.
cards.why: A Wednesday afternoon massage is an underrated move.

cards.name: Urban Plates
cards.address: 3055 Olin Ave #1020, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Stop by Urban Plates.
cards.why: Swapped in as a fresh pick for this slot.

cards.name: Homestead Bowl & The X Bar
cards.address: 20990 Homestead Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Cap the night at Homestead Bowl in Cupertino — grab a lane, grab a drink from the bar, and knock out a couple games without taking it too seriously.
cards.why: Bowling on a weeknight is always a better idea than it sounds.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours wandering through the Computer History Museum. The exhibits on early computing, the internet's origins, and Silicon Valley's rise are genuinely wild to walk through.
cards.why: It reads as dry but it never is. You'll end up staying longer than planned.

cards.name: DishDash
cards.address: DishDash, Sunnyvale
cards.blurb: Hit DishDash for lunch. The lamb shank and kabobs are the move, and the pita situation alone is worth the trip.
cards.why: It's consistently one of the best lunches you can have in Sunnyvale, full stop.

cards.name: On Courage, Turmoil, and the Transformative Power of Love with Marjan Kamali
cards.address: 585 Franklin St, Mountain View, CA 94041
cards.blurb: Hear author Marjan Kamali discuss love, courage, and transformation during an intimate talk.
cards.why: Free, thoughtful, and a nice way to spend an hour mid-afternoon.

cards.name: Birk's
cards.address: 3955 Freedom Cir, Santa Clara, CA 95054, USA
cards.blurb: Birk's is a proper steakhouse in Santa Clara. Go for a classic cut, settle into the dim lighting, and take your time with it.
cards.why: You've had a full day and this is exactly the kind of dinner that closes it out right.

cards.name: Meditative Drawing Workshop
cards.blurb: Learn simple line patterns inspired by Filipino tattoo and weaving traditions in this beginner-friendly workshop.
cards.why: It's a calm, creative way to wind down after dinner without straying too far from the South Bay.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a walk along the bay trail while the mist is still burning off. It's quiet this early and the light on the water is genuinely pretty.
cards.why: Perfect cool morning weather for a walk before the day fills up.

cards.name: Cupertino Farmers Market
cards.address: 21275 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino
cards.blurb: Shop for local produce, artisan goods, and ready-to-eat food weekly.
cards.why: Farmers market breakfast beats a drive-through every time.

cards.name: Picchetti Winery
cards.address: 13100 Montebello Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Wind back down Montebello to Picchetti Winery and settle in for a leisurely pour among the old-growth vines. The grounds here are beautiful and there's no rush to leave.
cards.why: Two historic wineries on the same road makes for a killer afternoon without any backtracking.

cards.name: The Spa at Four Seasons Hotel Silicon Valley
cards.address: 2050 University Ave, East Palo Alto, CA 94303, USA
cards.blurb: Decompress at the Four Seasons Spa with a treatment or just use the facilities. After a morning of walking and afternoon of wine, this is a genuinely excellent idea.
cards.why: You've earned it, and it's a Friday.
cards.venue: The Spa at Four Seasons Hotel Silicon Valley

cards.name: $9 Before 9 pm Happy Hour
cards.address: 48 South 1st Street, San Jose
cards.blurb: Grab discounted drinks and appetizers before 9 pm at Nomikai.
cards.why: Good drinks, low spend, and you're already out — easy yes.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Hit The Dish Trail for a brisk morning hike up into the Stanford foothills — the views from the top stretch across the whole valley and the cool morning air makes the climb genuinely enjoyable.
cards.why: 55 degrees at the start means ideal hiking conditions before it warms up.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Walk the Cantor Arts Center on Stanford's campus, including the outdoor Rodin sculpture garden — it's free and the collection is legitimately world-class.
cards.why: Free admission and a beautiful campus makes this one of the best no-cost stops in the whole South Bay.

cards.name: Zaytinya
cards.address: 180 El Camino Real Suite EE1400, Palo Alto, CA 94304, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Zaytinya for a Mediterranean lunch — share a spread of mezze and enjoy the indoor space at El Camino Real.
cards.why: It's a sharp lunch spot right in Palo Alto with food worth lingering over.

cards.name: Stanford Cardinal Baseball vs. North Carolina State Wolfpack Baseball
cards.address: 151 Sam McDonald Mall
cards.blurb: Watch Stanford baseball face North Carolina State in a college game.
cards.why: College baseball on a warm Saturday afternoon is honestly an underrated way to spend a few hours.

cards.name: Bookasaurus
cards.address: 125 S Murphy Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Browse Bookasaurus on Murphy Ave in Sunnyvale — it's a well-curated indie bookstore worth poking around after the game.
cards.why: A genuinely good indie bookstore is rare, and this one earns the stop.

cards.name: Preligen's Artisan Bakery
cards.address: 1597 Meridian Ave D, San Jose, CA 95125, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a fresh-baked pastry and a warm cup of coffee at this beloved neighborhood bakery known for its artisan breads and sweet treats.
cards.why: A cozy, low-key breakfast spot sets a relaxed tone before a full afternoon of wine, games, and culture.

cards.name: Los Gatos Farmers Market
cards.address: 50 University Ave, Los Gatos
cards.blurb: Browse fresh produce, artisan goods, and grab prepared food from local vendors.
cards.why: It lands right in Los Gatos, putting you close to Gali Vineyards before lunch with no backtracking needed.

cards.name: Gali Vineyards
cards.address: 21 W Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Gali Vineyards opens at noon and pours in an outdoor setting in Los Gatos — grab a glass and take your time with it.
cards.why: Los Gatos wine tasting on a crisp Sunday afternoon is a genuinely good use of an afternoon.

cards.name: Board Game Sunday!
cards.blurb: Play board games designed for all ages with friends and family on Sunday afternoons.
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: The Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum is genuinely weird in the best way — real mummies, actual ancient artifacts, and a full replica tomb you can walk through.
cards.why: You've probably driven past the signs a hundred times and never stopped — today's the day.

cards.name: Green Healthy Massage
cards.address: 2956 Scott Blvd, Santa Clara, CA 95054, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the day with a massage at Green Healthy Massage in Santa Clara — they're open late so there's no rush.
cards.why: After a full day of walking and wine tasting, this is exactly what you need.

cards.name: Rancho San Antonio Open Space
cards.address: Rancho San Antonio County Park
cards.blurb: Get out on the trails before the day heats up. The lower loop is an easy warm-up, or push up to the Deer Hollow Farm area if you want a bit more elevation.
cards.why: Cool morning air, no crowds yet, and it's completely free.

cards.name: Choc Cookies
cards.address: 1614 Pomeroy Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95051, USA
cards.blurb: Go-to spot nearby — easy table.
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: Naschmarkt Restaurant - Campbell
cards.address: 384 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Settle into Naschmarkt's cozy Austrian-inspired dining room and work through a leisurely lunch — the schnitzel and seasonal sides are worth taking your time with.
cards.why: A well-regarded Campbell spot that fits neatly between your cookie stop and the library without rushing the afternoon.

cards.name: Woodland Branch Library
cards.address: 1975 Grant Rd, Los Altos, CA 94024, USA
cards.blurb: Woodland Branch is a genuinely pleasant library to spend an hour browsing. Pick up something you've been meaning to read, flip through the new arrivals shelf, or just decompress in a quiet corner.
cards.why: A low-key afternoon stop that doesn't require you to spend money or be anywhere specific.

cards.name: Altitude Trampoline Park
cards.address: 147 Great Mall Dr, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Bounce between trampolines, foam pits, and climbing walls at this sprawling indoor park — a good way to shake out some energy before the evening.
cards.why: A fun, active break that fills the gap before Board Game Night without pulling you far from the Saratoga direction.

cards.name: Board Game Night for Adults
cards.blurb: Drop in between 6 and 8pm to play from a variety of board games with other adults.
cards.why: Free, social, and a fun way to close out a Monday.

cards.name: Los Gatos Farmers Market
cards.address: 50 University Ave, Los Gatos
cards.blurb: Browse fresh produce, artisan goods, and grab prepared food from local vendors.
cards.why: Los Gatos puts on a good market and the village vibe on a Sunday morning is hard to beat.

cards.name: Burrell School Vineyard and Winery
cards.address: 24060 Summit Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95033, USA
cards.blurb: Burrell School opens at noon and it's absolutely worth coming back for the tasting. Pour through their lineup on the outdoor patio with hilltop views that stretch way out.
cards.why: One of the best-regarded wineries in the South Bay and the setting is the whole experience.

cards.name: SJSU Theatre Presents: Kingdom of Cards
cards.blurb: Watch a modern adaptation of Tagore's play about power and rigid societal rules.
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll the lakeside path, watch the ducks drift by, and find a sunny bench to decompress before the evening.
cards.why: It's a relaxed outdoor reset between the afternoon show and dinner, and it's right in the Los Gatos area where you already are.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Wander through ancient Egyptian artifacts, mummies, and replica tomb chambers inside this quirky San Jose gem.
cards.why: It bridges the gap before San Pedro Square perfectly and keeps you in the San Jose area without any doubling back.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at San Pedro Square Market in downtown San Jose. Grab tacos, ramen, banh mi — whatever sounds right — and eat outside on the patio with a drink.
cards.why: Easy, low-key, and lets you graze your way through dinner without committing to one thing.

cards.name: Daily Donuts & Sandwiches
cards.address: 708 Fair Oaks Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94085, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a fresh donut and coffee at this no-fuss spot before the day kicks off.
cards.why: Cheap, fast, and exactly the kind of place you want at 7:30 on a Tuesday.

cards.name: JoyFull: Daily Rituals to Eat Freely and Live Radiantly with Radhi Devlukia-Shetty
cards.address: 585 Franklin St, Mountain View, CA 94041
cards.blurb: Hear Radhi Devlukia-Shetty discuss intuitive eating and wellness practices for daily life.
cards.why: It's free, it's a Tuesday morning, and this is genuinely the kind of thing worth showing up for.

cards.name: Anchors Fish & Chips And Seafood Grill
cards.address: 14441 Big Basin Wy, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Anchors on Big Basin Way for fish and chips or a seafood plate — classic casual lunch on Saratoga's main drag.
cards.why: Good food, solid vibe, and it's the kind of spot that doesn't feel like a chain.
cards.venue: Anchors Fish & Chips And Seafood Grill

cards.name: Panel Discussion: The Middle East in a Year
cards.blurb: Hear three SCU professors debate the Middle East's trajectory and geopolitical shifts this year.
cards.why: Free, thought-provoking, and a solid way to spend a late afternoon.

cards.name: Homeridge Park
cards.address: 2985 Stevenson St, Santa Clara, CA 95051, USA
cards.blurb: Stretch your legs at this quiet neighborhood park in Santa Clara — good spot to decompress with a walk or just sit and enjoy the mild afternoon air after a full day of events.
cards.why: It's a low-key outdoor reset between the panel discussion and your evening plans.

cards.name: Saratoga Library
cards.address: 13650 Saratoga Ave, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at Saratoga Library's evening hours, a quietly great spot to browse, sit, and decompress after a full day.
cards.why: Open late, calm, and a genuinely pleasant way to close out the evening.

cards.name: 7 Leaves Cafe
cards.address: 1743 Berryessa Rd, San Jose, CA 95131, USA
cards.blurb: 7 Leaves does Vietnamese-inspired drinks and light bites — grab a taro milk tea or a matcha latte to kick off the morning.
cards.why: Good coffee-shop energy without the usual chain scene.

cards.name: Music@Noon – Poetry in Music
cards.blurb: Hear singer-songwriter Jim Infantino perform poetry set to music at noon.
cards.why: Free live music on a mild Wednesday afternoon is a pretty easy yes.

cards.name: Main Street Cupertino
cards.address: 19401 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the outdoor plaza at Main Street Cupertino, browse the shops, and grab a late snack or bubble tea from one of the Asian spots along the strip.
cards.why: It's a relaxed way to spend the afternoon without committing to anything heavy.

cards.name: DishDash
cards.address: DishDash, Sunnyvale
cards.blurb: Start the morning at DishDash with some strong coffee and a warm breakfast before the day kicks off.
cards.why: It's a Sunnyvale staple and a solid way to ease into a Thursday.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Walk through the Computer History Museum's main exhibit and see everything from the first hard drives to early internet hardware. The Revolution exhibit alone takes a solid hour.
cards.why: Even if you're not a tech person, the stories here are genuinely wild.

cards.name: Oren's Hummus
cards.address: 261 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Oren's Hummus on University Ave. The hummus is silky, the pitas are fresh, and the whole spread comes fast.
cards.why: One of the best lunch values in the area and always worth the trip.

cards.name: Sandy Wool Lake - Gazebo
cards.address: 901 Downing Rd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Stretch your legs around Sandy Wool Lake and take in the gazebo views. It's a calm, flat loop that's great on a cool afternoon.
cards.why: Low-key and pretty, exactly what a Thursday afternoon calls for.

cards.name: Altitude Trampoline Park
cards.address: 147 Great Mall Dr, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Bounce around on trampolines and unwind with some high-energy fun before the evening.
cards.why: A lively way to burn off energy between the afternoon lake visit and heading to Saratoga for the night.

cards.name: Saratoga Nights: 1st Thursday of the Month
cards.blurb: Browse art and vintage vendors, grab dinner, and hear live music under the stars.
cards.why: It's free, it's outside, and the village vibe on a Thursday night is hard to beat.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Walk or bike the loop around Shoreline Lake with the Bay spread out in front of you. The morning air at 55°F is perfect for moving around before it warms up.
cards.why: Cool temps and wide open waterfront views make this a great way to start the day.

cards.name: The Tech Interactive
cards.address: 201 S Market St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Dive into hands-on science and technology exhibits spanning robotics, biotech, and innovation — there's plenty to explore across multiple floors.
cards.why: A great way to keep the morning momentum going with something engaging before lunch.

cards.name: SJSU Student Union Bowling Center
cards.address: 211 S 9th St, San Jose, CA 95192, USA
cards.blurb: Bowl a few frames at this lively campus bowling center with a fun, laid-back atmosphere perfect for an afternoon hangout.
cards.why: It's a low-key, entertaining way to spend the afternoon before heading into the evening.

cards.name: Coterie Winery
cards.address: 885 W Julian St, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Coterie is a boutique winery with evening hours that feel like a private hangout. Good pours in a laid-back setting before dinner.
cards.why: An easy, unhurried way to bridge afternoon into evening.

cards.name: $9 Before 9 pm Happy Hour
cards.address: 48 South 1st Street, San Jose
cards.blurb: Grab discounted drinks and appetizers before 9 pm at Nomikai.
cards.why: A smart way to keep the good vibes going after the winery without spending much.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Walk or jog the 3.5-mile loop through Stanford's rolling foothills, passing the iconic dish antenna with sweeping views of the Bay and Peninsula.
cards.why: A breezy morning hike is the perfect way to shake off the early chill before the day fills up.

cards.name: Sunnyvale Farmers Market
cards.address: Murphy Ave, Sunnyvale
cards.blurb: Shop for local produce, artisan goods, and ready-to-eat food weekly.
cards.why: It's a relaxed way to grab breakfast on the go and soak in some weekend market energy before heading to the museum.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Walk the Rodin sculpture garden, then duck inside for the rotating galleries — the permanent collection alone is worth a full hour without feeling rushed.
cards.why: Free world-class art on Stanford's campus is always a good call.

cards.name: Cafe Nur
cards.address: 280 Main St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Settle in at Cafe Nur on Main Street in Los Altos for a Mediterranean lunch, the kind of spot where you linger over good food and don't feel rushed out the door.
cards.why: It's a genuinely great neighborhood restaurant that earns its reputation on the food alone.

cards.name: Asian American Stories Award Ceremony 2026
cards.address: Mission City Center for Performing Arts, 3250 Monroe St, Santa Clara, California 95051
cards.blurb: Watch cultural performances, hear Asian American storytellers, bid in an auction, and network with community leaders.
cards.why: It's a compelling mix of community and culture that you don't come across on a random Saturday.
cards.venue: Mission City Center for Performing Arts

cards.name: Co Ba Dessert, Tea & Coffee
cards.address: 3730 N First St Ste. 110, San Jose, CA 95134, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down with Vietnamese-inspired desserts, specialty teas, and creative coffee drinks in a cozy, warmly lit café space.
cards.why: A sweet, low-key finish near Santa Clara after the award ceremony — exactly what you need after a full day out.

cards.name: Stevens Creek Trail
cards.address: Unnamed Road, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a walk along Stevens Creek Trail before the crowds show up. It's flat, scenic, and a genuinely nice way to shake off sleep when it's still cool out.
cards.why: Crisp morning air and a mellow pace is exactly the right vibe at this hour.

cards.name: Mountain View Farmers Market
cards.address: 600 W Evelyn Ave, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Shop for local produce, artisan goods, and ready-to-eat food weekly.
cards.why: It's one of the better Sunday markets in the South Bay and lines up perfectly after the trail.

cards.name: Academy of Classical Ballet: The Four Seasons and Peter and the Wolf
cards.address: Heritage Theatre, 1 W. Campbell Ave #C32, Campbell, CA 95008
cards.blurb: Watch dancers perform two one-act ballets including Vivaldi's Four Seasons and Peter and the Wolf.
cards.why: Live ballet on a Sunday afternoon is a low-key elevated move and Heritage Theatre is an intimate venue.

cards.name: Yvette Spa by Jackie
cards.address: 248 W Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Book a treatment at Yvette Spa by Jackie on West Main in Los Gatos and decompress after the afternoon. It's a small, well-regarded massage spa, so go ahead and treat yourself.
cards.why: A post-ballet spa hour is a genuinely good Sunday wind-down.

cards.name: Rancho San Antonio Open Space
cards.address: Rancho San Antonio County Park
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a hike through Rancho San Antonio — the lower trails are wide and easy, or push up to the ridge for real views once the fog burns off.
cards.why: 55 degrees at sunrise is genuinely perfect hiking weather.

cards.name: Gourmet Works
cards.address: 334 Main St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Pop into Gourmet Works on Main Street for a post-hike treat — their chocolate selection is serious, and it's a great excuse to wander the Los Altos downtown strip.
cards.why: You've earned something sweet after the trail.

cards.name: Palo Alto Art Center
cards.address: 1313 Newell Rd, Palo Alto, CA 94303, USA
cards.blurb: The Palo Alto Art Center has free rotating exhibitions worth a slow walk through — good natural light, interesting contemporary work, and no crowds on a Monday morning.
cards.why: Arts has been light this week and this is one of the better free gallery spaces in the South Bay.

cards.name: J. Lohr Vineyards & Wines
cards.address: 1000 Lenzen Ave, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: J. Lohr is a legit tasting room stop for lunch-hour wine — settle in, do a flight, and take your time with it.
cards.why: A 4.9-rated winery on a Monday means you'll basically have the place to yourself.

cards.name: K Spa and Bodywork
cards.address: 151 2nd St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage at K Spa and Bodywork and let the post-hike soreness actually get worked out — it's a proper spa, not a quick chair massage situation.
cards.why: Wellness has barely shown up this week and your legs probably agree it's time.

cards.name: Anton
cards.address: 1969 O'Toole Way, San Jose, CA 95131, USA
cards.blurb: Anton is a bakery worth stopping at in the late afternoon — grab a pastry or a bite to hold you over before dinner.
cards.why: Good bridge between the spa and the evening without committing to a full sit-down meal yet.

cards.name: Science Fiction and Fantasy Book Club - Doomsday Book by Connie Willis
cards.blurb: Read Doomsday Book by Connie Willis and discuss the science fiction novel with fellow book club members.
cards.why: Free, low-key, and a genuinely great sci-fi novel to talk about on a Monday night.

cards.name: Heroes
cards.address: 24 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Browse the shelves at Heroes, a comic and pop culture bookstore on Campbell Ave with a solid mix of graphic novels, back issues, and collectibles — easy to lose an hour in here.
cards.why: It's a fun, low-key morning stop with zero pressure to buy anything.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Head to the Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum for real mummies, hieroglyphics, and artifacts you genuinely wouldn't expect to find in San Jose. The planetarium and garden grounds are worth a lap too.
cards.why: It's one of the most underrated places in the whole South Bay and way more interesting than it sounds.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: San Pedro Square Market is the move for lunch — grab a taco from one vendor, maybe ramen or banh mi from another, and find a patio seat to eat in the sun. Treat it like a food hall crawl.
cards.why: No commitment to a single menu, which is exactly what you want after a museum.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: The Computer History Museum is worth every minute. The main Revolution exhibit takes you from room-sized computers to the iPhone era and somehow makes all of it feel wild. Go at your own pace.
cards.why: The 71-degree afternoon is perfect for an indoor stretch, and this exhibit is genuinely hard to rush through.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll the lakeside paths, watch the ducks, and unwind on the grass as the afternoon cools down.
cards.why: A peaceful outdoor wind-down between the museum and your evening massage fits the day's easy-going vibe perfectly.

cards.name: Perfect healing massage center（836 W El Camino Real, Sunnyvale 94087)
cards.address: 836 W El Camino Real, Sunnyvale, CA 94087, USA
cards.blurb: Wind the night down with a massage at Perfect Healing in Sunnyvale. Book ahead if you can, but walk-ins are often welcome. A solid 60 or 90 minute session is the right call after a full day.
cards.why: There's no better way to close out a Sunday than actually relaxing.
cards.venue: Perfect healing massage center（836 W El Camino Real, Sunnyvale 94087)

cards.name: Peet's Coffee
cards.address: 22350 Homestead Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a walk through Peet's before the day picks up — grab a latte and a pastry and ease into it.
cards.why: A solid cup of coffee on a crisp 54-degree morning sets the whole day up right.

cards.name: Mount Eden Vineyards
cards.address: 22020 Mt Eden Rd, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Head up to Mount Eden Vineyards for a tasting — it's a mountain winery with sweeping views and wines that earn their reputation. They open at 10, so you'll get in early before the crowds.
cards.why: A Tuesday morning wine tasting on a hillside in Saratoga is genuinely hard to beat.

cards.name: Resume & Cover Letter Workshop
cards.address: 150  E San Fernando St San Jose
cards.blurb: Learn to craft a competitive resume and cover letter from a career librarian.
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: Ridge Vineyards
cards.address: 17100 Montebello Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Ridge Vineyards is the crown jewel of the Montebello road — taste through their estate Chardonnay and Cabernet blends on the terrace with views that stretch to the bay on clear days.
cards.why: Ridge is one of the most celebrated American wineries and it's right there on the same road.

cards.name: Main Street Cupertino
cards.address: 19401 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll the open-air shops and grab a bite at one of the casual spots along Stevens Creek Blvd before the evening gets going.
cards.why: A low-key way to decompress between the winery and dinner without straying far from Cupertino.

cards.name: Sandbaggers Golf Spot
cards.address: 2292 Quimby Rd, San Jose, CA 95122, USA
cards.blurb: Step into the bays and work on your swing with a guided hitting experience at this well-reviewed golf spot in San Jose.
cards.why: A fun, active way to cap the evening that's a nice change of pace after a day of food and culture.

cards.name: Tylan cafe
cards.address: 2056 N Capitol Ave, San Jose, CA 95132, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Tylan Cafe with coffee and something to eat before the day gets going.
cards.why: Solid cafe with good hours and a relaxed vibe to ease into the morning.

cards.name: Zoning Administrator Hearing
cards.blurb: Attend a public hearing on zoning requests and land-use decisions.
cards.why: If you care about what gets built in your backyard, this is how you actually find out.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Walk through the Triton Museum of Art and spend some time in the sculpture garden out back. Free admission, rotating contemporary exhibits, and way more chill than a big city museum.
cards.why: A good way to shift gears after the hike without spending a dime.

cards.name: Taplands
cards.address: 1171 Homestead Rd, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Taplands opens at 3 and has a rotating selection of craft beers on tap. Grab a pint, find a spot, and decompress.
cards.why: It's a Thursday afternoon and you've earned a beer.

cards.name: Fox Hole Golf Club
cards.address: 545 Aldo Ave #13, Santa Clara, CA 95054, USA
cards.blurb: Knock a few balls around at this quirky indoor golf spot — a low-key way to unwind between dinner and the game.
cards.why: It's a fun, easy stop to bridge the gap before heading to the San Jose Giants.

cards.name: San Jose Giants vs. Fresno Grizzlies
cards.address: 588 E. Alma Avenue
cards.blurb: Cheer on the San Jose Giants against Fresno Grizzlies.
cards.why: Minor league baseball on a weeknight is an underrated Thursday move.

cards.name: Jack Fischer Park
cards.address: Abbott Ave & Pollard Road, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Jack Fischer Park is a solid morning hangout — walk the loop, find a bench, and shake off the morning slow.
cards.why: Cool air, no crowds, and a genuinely pleasant place to start the day.

cards.name: Santana Row Farmers Market
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose
cards.blurb: Shop for local produce, artisan goods, and ready-to-eat food weekly.
cards.why: Wednesday market, good weather, and the kind of slow browse that actually feels good mid-morning.

cards.name: Gali Vineyards
cards.address: 21 W Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Go-to spot nearby — easy table.
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: LA ViE MD Los Gatos
cards.address: 652 N Santa Cruz Ave Suite A, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: LA ViE MD in Los Gatos is a solid wind-down spot after a full day out, with spa services to close things out properly.
cards.why: A relaxed Wednesday evening calls for something that feels a little indulgent.

cards.name: Luna Mexican Kitchen
cards.address: Luna Mexican Kitchen, Campbell
cards.blurb: Order tacos and a margarita on the patio at Luna Mexican Kitchen. Low-key, good food, and the kind of place that doesn't rush you.
cards.why: Campbell's best easygoing dinner spot, and the patio is exactly right on a mild night like this.

cards.name: Homestead Bowl & The X Bar
cards.address: 20990 Homestead Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap the night with a few frames at Homestead Bowl. The X Bar is on-site if you'd rather drink than bowl.
cards.why: Late bowling on a Wednesday is exactly the kind of low-effort fun that actually delivers.

cards.name: Dakao Sandwiches II
cards.address: 72 S Abel St, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a banh mi or sandwich early before the morning rush hits. Quick, cheap, and genuinely good.
cards.why: Best under-$15 breakfast move in Milpitas.

cards.name: Week 1: Safe Homes, Strong Communities
cards.blurb: Join the community workshop on creating safer homes and stronger neighborhoods.
cards.why: Good way to meet locals and spend a free morning productively.

cards.name: Big Al's
cards.address: 27 Ranch Dr, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Settle into the patio at Big Al's for lunch. Solid spot to decompress after the morning with a meal and a drink in the open air.
cards.why: The outdoor seating works well on a mild day like this.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the sculpture garden and browse the contemporary galleries at Triton. Free admission, genuinely impressive collection for a regional museum.
cards.why: Free, beautiful, and a totally different vibe from the morning.

cards.name: Saigon Spa
cards.address: 2055 Grant Rd Ste 100, Los Altos, CA 94024, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage at Saigon Spa and let the afternoon melt off. Good way to transition from sightseeing mode into evening mode.
cards.why: You'll feel like a different person by dinner.

cards.name: Fremont Older Open Space
cards.address: Fremont Older Open Space Preserve
cards.blurb: Get out to Fremont Older Open Space for a morning hike while the trails are still quiet. The foothill views are legitimately great and you'll likely have the place mostly to yourself on a Wednesday.
cards.why: This is the kind of Cupertino gem most people drive past without knowing it exists.

cards.name: Music@Noon – Poetry in Music
cards.blurb: Hear singer-songwriter Jim Infantino perform poetry set to music at noon.
cards.why: You're already there, it starts right at noon, and it makes for a genuinely different kind of lunch break.

cards.name: Fox Hole Golf Club
cards.address: 545 Aldo Ave #13, Santa Clara, CA 95054, USA
cards.blurb: Fox Hole Golf Club in Santa Clara does simulator-style indoor golf, great for a casual afternoon round without the full-course commitment. Grab a bay and play at your own pace.
cards.why: It's a fun low-key afternoon activity that doesn't need perfect weather to work.

cards.name: Main Street Cupertino
cards.address: 19401 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Wind up the evening at Main Street Cupertino. Browse the outdoor plaza, pick one of the many restaurants for dinner, and linger as long as you want. The area stays lively on weekday evenings.
cards.why: It's Cupertino's best evening hang and a comfortable way to close out the day.

cards.name: Xiang Xiang Noodle
cards.address: 1115 Tasman Dr #2228, Sunnyvale, CA 94089, USA
cards.blurb: Settle in for a steaming bowl of hand-pulled noodles and rich broth — the perfect sit-down dinner to refuel after a full afternoon out.
cards.why: Solid noodles at a reasonable price make this an easy dinner call while you're still in the flow of the evening.

cards.name: 1 Oz Coffee
cards.address: 549 Benton St, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at this cozy Santa Clara coffee shop — grab a well-made espresso drink and settle in before the day gets going.
cards.why: Good coffee at a place that actually cares about what they're serving.

cards.name: Japanese Paper Arts Workshop
cards.blurb: Create the Awaji Knot using Mizuhiki Japanese paper cords, a 1500-year-old decorative craft technique.
cards.why: Free, creative, and something you've almost certainly never tried before.

cards.name: DishDash
cards.address: DishDash, Sunnyvale
cards.blurb: Head to DishDash for lunch — the lamb shank and kabobs are the move here, and the Middle Eastern flavors hit every time.
cards.why: It's one of Sunnyvale's most dependable lunch spots and the food is genuinely great.

cards.name: Bookasaurus
cards.address: 125 S Murphy Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Pop into Bookasaurus on Murphy Ave and browse the shelves — it's the kind of independent bookstore where you always leave with something you didn't expect to buy.
cards.why: A low-key afternoon detour that's hard to walk out of empty-handed.

cards.name: On Courage, Turmoil, and the Transformative Power of Love with Marjan Kamali
cards.address: 585 Franklin St, Mountain View, CA 94041
cards.blurb: Hear author Marjan Kamali discuss love, courage, and transformation during an intimate talk.
cards.why: Arts and conversation are exactly what a Thursday evening calls for.

cards.name: Gali Vineyards
cards.address: 21 W Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Gali Vineyards for a glass of wine on the way back — it's a relaxed pour-and-unwind kind of spot perfect for early evening.
cards.why: A 4.8-rated wine stop with evening hours is a solid transition into dinner.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Stretch your legs along the lakeside trails and take in the cool morning air before the day fills up with food and wine.
cards.why: A peaceful start right in Mountain View before you head toward Cupertino.

cards.name: Cupertino Farmers Market
cards.address: 21275 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino
cards.blurb: Shop for local produce, artisan goods, and ready-to-eat food weekly.
cards.why: It's free, it's lively on a Friday morning, and the food vendors make it easy to eat well without sitting down anywhere.

cards.name: Vidovich Vineyards
cards.address: 18101 Montebello Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Vidovich Vineyards sits up on Montebello Road and pours estate wines in a relaxed outdoor setting. Grab a glass on the patio and take in the hillside views.
cards.why: You're already up in the Cupertino hills, so stopping here on the way back down just makes sense.

cards.name: Picchetti Ranch
cards.address: 13100 Montebello Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Wander the historic ranch trails through oak woodlands and rolling hills, with views that open up beautifully on a clear afternoon.
cards.why: A natural outdoor breather between your midday wine stop and the Los Gatos dinner, and it's just minutes from both.

cards.name: PintxoPote
cards.address: 424 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: PintxoPote on North Santa Cruz Ave does Basque-style tapas that are meant for sharing. Order a spread of small plates and a bottle of txakoli and take your time with it.
cards.why: It's a great Friday night dinner spot with the kind of food that actually rewards lingering.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Hit the Stanford Foothills trail loop up to The Dish — the climb is steady but the panoramic views of the Bay and peninsula make it worth every step.
cards.why: Perfect morning temps for this hike before it warms up.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Walk the Cantor Arts Center on Stanford's campus, including the outdoor Rodin sculpture garden — free world-class art with no ticket needed.
cards.why: You're already on campus and this is genuinely one of the best free museums in the Bay.

cards.name: Gallery Conversations | Jeremy Frey: Woven
cards.address: 328 Lomita Drive, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Discuss woven textiles and art directly with curators in the gallery.
cards.why: Arts has been light this week and this kind of direct curator access is rare.

cards.name: Athena Grill & Catering
cards.address: 1505 Space Park Dr, Santa Clara, CA 95054, USA
cards.blurb: Drive over to Athena Grill in Santa Clara for a proper Greek lunch — think rotisserie meats, mezze, and something warm after the morning hike.
cards.why: Solid mid-afternoon lunch spot that won't rush you out the door.

cards.name: 1,000 Ways to Hold: A New Work By Erika Chong Shuch
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: See an interactive clay and conversation installation exploring ways of holding and connection.
cards.why: It's free, thought-provoking, and wraps up before the 5 PM cutoff.

cards.name: Campbell Farmers Market
cards.address: Campbell Ave, Campbell
cards.blurb: Shop for local produce, artisan goods, and ready-to-eat food weekly.
cards.why: Sunday morning markets are the move when the weather's this crisp and mild.

cards.name: Perrucci Family Vineyard
cards.address: 15050 Kennedy Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Perrucci Family Vineyard sits up in the Los Gatos hills — show up, pour a glass, and take in the outdoor setting at your own pace.
cards.why: A low-key afternoon pour beats sitting inside on a Sunday like this.

cards.name: Alum Rock Park
cards.address: 15350 Penitencia Creek Rd, San Jose, CA 95127, USA
cards.blurb: Good place to walk and clear your head.
cards.why: A hidden gem in the foothills — peaceful and free, great for families and solo explorers

cards.name: Arawan Thai Massage and Spa
cards.address: 14107 Winchester Blvd Suite J, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Slip in for a quick targeted massage or foot treatment to recharge your legs after a morning of market browsing and hiking.
cards.why: A short wellness reset between the vineyard and the park keeps you energized for the rest of the day.

cards.name: Iraklis Restaurant
cards.address: 2165 El Camino Real, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Loop back to Iraklis for a proper sit-down dinner — the Mediterranean menu works just as well for dinner as it does for a morning stop.
cards.why: Solid food, easy price point, and a good way to wind down after an afternoon in the hills.

cards.name: Stevens Creek Trail
cards.address: Unnamed Road, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll or bike along this flat, paved trail as the evening cools down, passing creekside greenery and open sky.
cards.why: It's a relaxed way to wind down after dinner without straying far from Santa Clara.

cards.name: Monday Morning Meditation & Mindfulness
cards.blurb: Practice guided meditation for 20 minutes, then ask questions with instructor Manisha.
cards.why: A Monday reset that costs nothing and takes less than an hour.

cards.name: Los Altos Grill
cards.address: 233 3rd St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Los Altos Grill for lunch. It's a solid American spot on 3rd Street, good for a proper sit-down meal with a drink.
cards.why: It's right in Los Altos and the food holds up.

cards.name: Rancho San Antonio Open Space
cards.address: Rancho San Antonio County Park
cards.blurb: Take advantage of the afternoon warmth at Rancho San Antonio. The upper trails get you views without being brutal, and the valley loop is an easy walk if you want something mellower.
cards.why: 70 degrees and a weekday afternoon means the trails won't be packed.

cards.name: Illusive Comics & Games, LLC
cards.address: 5245 Stevens Creek Blvd, Santa Clara, CA 95051, USA
cards.blurb: Browse Illusive Comics and Games in Santa Clara. It's a proper comic shop with a deep back catalog, board games, and the kind of staff who actually know what they're talking about.
cards.why: Great way to wind down after a hike without completely stopping.

cards.name: Naschmarkt - Palo Alto
cards.address: 2323 Birch St, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Naschmarkt in Palo Alto does Austrian food, which is not something you see often. The schnitzel is the obvious move, but the whole menu is worth a look.
cards.why: It's genuinely different from the usual South Bay dinner rotation.

cards.name: Mitchell Park Library
cards.address: 3700 Middlefield Rd, Palo Alto, CA 94303, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the evening browsing Mitchell Park Library's well-stocked shelves, from new fiction to local history — a quiet, welcoming space to decompress after a full day.
cards.why: A calm, no-cost way to close out the evening right in Palo Alto after dinner.

cards.name: Fremont Older Open Space
cards.address: Fremont Older Open Space Preserve
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with a walk or easy hike through rolling oak-studded hills and open grasslands with sweeping views of the valley.
cards.why: A peaceful way to ease into the day before the arts and wine stops take over the afternoon.

cards.name: Jonathan Calm - To Wherever, Forever: Archives of Absence & Sites of Passage
cards.blurb: Explore two linked exhibitions examining absence, memory, and migration through art.
cards.why: Free art that's thoughtful and not crowded on a Wednesday morning.

cards.name: Ridge Vineyards
cards.address: 17100 Montebello Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Ridge is open now — taste through their estate Chardonnay and Monte Bello Cabernet on the outdoor patio with vineyard views in every direction.
cards.why: Ridge is one of the genuinely great California wineries and the patio on a 70-degree afternoon is hard to beat.

cards.name: Grieving Demolition: Grappling with Chávez’s Tarnished Legacy
cards.blurb: Join a panel discussion examining Chávez's legacy and its complicated historical significance.
cards.why: It's free, it's substantive, and it's a good way to end the afternoon before dinner.

cards.name: Main Street Cupertino
cards.address: 19401 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the outdoor plaza at Main Street Cupertino, pick one of the Asian restaurants for dinner, and enjoy the mild evening air steps from Apple Park.
cards.why: Cupertino's best casual dinner stretch and a nice way to wind down the evening.

cards.name: Homestead Bowl & The X Bar
cards.address: 20990 Homestead Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Homestead Bowl has lanes open late and The X Bar attached for drinks between frames — grab a lane, order something cold, and keep it loose.
cards.why: Good low-key Wednesday night energy without having to go far.

cards.name: Braly Park
cards.address: 704 Daffodil Ct, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a walk through Braly Park while the air is still crisp. Good path to shake off the early hour before the day kicks in.
cards.why: A cool 55-degree morning is basically perfect for a quiet park walk.

cards.name: Ethics Certificate Training for SCU Staff and Students - May 7 & 14, 2026
cards.blurb: Work through real ethical dilemmas with trained facilitators across two sessions to sharpen your decision-making.
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: DishDash
cards.address: DishDash, Sunnyvale
cards.blurb: DishDash in Sunnyvale is the move for lunch. The lamb shank and kabobs are the go-to orders, and it's always buzzing in the best way.
cards.why: Solid Middle Eastern food that holds up every single time.

cards.name: Alum Rock Park
cards.address: 15350 Penitencia Creek Rd, San Jose, CA 95127, USA
cards.blurb: Good place to walk and clear your head.
cards.why: A hidden gem in the foothills — peaceful and free, great for families and solo explorers

cards.name: Saratoga Nights: 1st Thursday of the Month
cards.blurb: Browse art and vintage vendors, grab dinner, and hear live music under the stars.
cards.why: Free street event with actual good energy, not just a farmers market vibe.

cards.name: Yiassoo
cards.address: 10660 S De Anza Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the evening with warm Greek plates — think flavorful spreads, grilled meats, and fresh pita in a cozy neighborhood spot.
cards.why: A satisfying dinner close to your Saratoga stop that keeps the evening relaxed without backtracking far.

cards.name: Manley’s Donuts
cards.address: 2590 S Bascom Ave, San Jose, CA 95124, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Manley's with a fresh-glazed donut or two. Classic South Bay donut shop energy, open early, no fuss.
cards.why: Hard to beat a $5 donut breakfast when the morning is still cool and quiet.

cards.name: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.address: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.blurb: Wander the Japanese garden terraces at Hakone while the morning light is still soft. The bamboo grove and koi ponds are the main draw, so take your time and just meander.
cards.why: This is genuinely one of the most peaceful spots in the whole South Bay.

cards.name: Ridge Vineyards
cards.address: 17100 Montebello Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Head up to Ridge Vineyards on Montebello Road for a tasting on the hilltop deck. The views over the valley are ridiculous and the pours are serious.
cards.why: Few places let you drink great wine outdoors with that kind of scenery on a Tuesday.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Browse the galleries and sculpture garden at the Triton Museum in Santa Clara. The collection rotates regularly and the outdoor sculpture space is genuinely worth lingering in.
cards.why: It's free and consistently one of the most underrated art spaces in the region.

cards.name: ShirSpa
cards.address: 15868 Cherry Blossom Ln, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the afternoon with a relaxing spa treatment at ShirSpa, a serene spot tucked in Los Gatos perfect for shedding any museum-hopping fatigue before dinner.
cards.why: A mellow wellness stop bridges the gap nicely between the art museum and the evening concert.

cards.name: Sax Ensemble
cards.blurb: Hear a sax ensemble perform live at San Jose State's Music Building.
cards.why: Ending a Tuesday night with live saxophone is a solid move.

cards.name: Jonathan Calm - To Wherever, Forever: Archives of Absence & Sites of Passage
cards.blurb: Explore two linked exhibitions examining absence, memory, and migration through art.
cards.why: Free, thought-provoking, and a genuinely different way to spend a Wednesday morning.

cards.name: J. Lohr Vineyards & Wines
cards.address: 1000 Lenzen Ave, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by J. Lohr for a noon tasting on the outdoor patio. Pour through their reds and whites at your own pace with no rush.
cards.why: The outdoor setting and the San Jose location make this a laid-back midday stop worth lingering over.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll along this flat, scenic creekside path winding through Campbell — great for a relaxed midday walk before the afternoon picks up.
cards.why: A breezy outdoor stretch is the perfect way to shake off a wine-tasting lunch before heading into shopping and golf.

cards.name: Main Street Cupertino
cards.address: 19401 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Walk a lap around Main Street Cupertino as the afternoon cools off, browse the shops, and grab a drink or snack at one of the outdoor spots in the plaza.
cards.why: A low-key way to decompress before dinner without committing to anything.

cards.name: Fox Hole Golf Club
cards.address: 545 Aldo Ave #13, Santa Clara, CA 95054, USA
cards.blurb: Book a bay at Fox Hole Golf Club in Santa Clara for a couple hours of simulator golf. No tee time stress, just pick your course and go.
cards.why: Indoor golf on a Wednesday evening with a drink in hand is a seriously underrated move.

cards.name: Oren's Hummus
cards.address: 261 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Good food, no fuss.
cards.why: The hummus is worth a trip to Palo Alto — fast, fresh, and one of the best lunch values on the Peninsula

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Take a walk or bike ride along the Bay Trail at Shoreline. The morning light on the water is genuinely nice at this hour, and the open space feels good when the weather is this mild.
cards.why: Cool crisp morning with a high of 69 is basically perfect for this.

cards.name: The Tech Interactive
cards.address: 201 S Market St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Explore hands-on science and tech exhibits across multiple floors — great for curious adults who like to tinker, play, and actually learn something.
cards.why: It fills the midday gap nicely and keeps you in the San Jose orbit where your evening is already anchored.

cards.name: Fox Hole Golf Club
cards.address: 545 Aldo Ave #13, Santa Clara, CA 95054, USA
cards.blurb: Fox Hole Golf Club has indoor golf simulators, so you can hit a full round or just mess around and compete with whoever you're with.
cards.why: Way more fun than it sounds, especially if you've never done simulator golf before.

cards.name: $9 Before 9 pm Happy Hour
cards.address: 48 South 1st Street, San Jose
cards.blurb: Grab discounted drinks and appetizers before 9 pm at Nomikai.
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: DishDash
cards.address: DishDash, Sunnyvale
cards.blurb: Start the morning with something warm and satisfying. The kabobs and lamb dishes are the crowd favorites here, and the vibe is relaxed enough to ease into the day.
cards.why: DishDash is a Sunnyvale staple and a great way to kick off a local day.

cards.name: Picchetti Winery
cards.address: 13100 Montebello Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Picchetti is one of those places where you grab a glass, find a picnic table, and just sit with the hillside views for a while. The peacocks wandering the property are a bonus you didn't ask for but will appreciate.
cards.why: 55 and sunny is exactly what this outdoor winery was made for.

cards.name: Vidovich Vineyards
cards.address: 18101 Montebello Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Vidovich is a small family winery tucked into the Montebello hills. Pour yourself a taste, walk the property, and take in the kind of quiet you don't get on the valley floor.
cards.why: Both Cupertino wineries sit on the same mountain road, so you can flow from one to the other without any effort.

cards.name: San Francisco Spring Happy Hour
cards.blurb: Grab drinks with fellow Santa Clara alumni and toast the season at a casual spring mixer.
cards.why: It's free, it's close, and it bridges the gap between afternoon and dinner perfectly.

cards.name: Patterned Flower Drawing for Adults and Teens
cards.blurb: Draw patterned flowers using simple line techniques in this beginner-friendly workshop with Kenneth Tan.
cards.why: A low-key creative session is a nice way to wind down after a day of wine and happy hour.

cards.name: House of Bagels
cards.address: 1712-D Miramonte Ave, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at House of Bagels in Mountain View. Grab a fresh bagel with your spread of choice and a coffee while the day warms up.
cards.why: It's a classic South Bay breakfast move and they're open early.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Hit The Dish Trail from the Stanford Ave trailhead. It's a steady climb with sweeping views of the Bay and foothills — worth pushing to the top before the afternoon crowd shows up.
cards.why: High 68°F is perfect hiking weather and the views from the ridge are genuinely great.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Walk through Cantor Arts Center on Stanford's campus. The Rodin sculptures in the outdoor garden are a highlight, and the rotating galleries inside are always worth a look.
cards.why: Free world-class art right on campus, and it's a natural next stop after the hike.

cards.name: Oren's Hummus
cards.address: 261 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Oren's Hummus on University Ave for lunch. Order the hummus plate, add a side or two, and eat well without overthinking it.
cards.why: It's one of the best lunch values on the Peninsula and you'll leave satisfied.

cards.name: Restaurant Gish
cards.address: 1998 Homestead Rd #113, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Drive over to Restaurant Gish in Santa Clara for dinner. The menu skews creative and the kitchen takes it seriously, so try the local favorite dishes and let the evening slow down.
cards.why: It punches above its weight for a Saturday night dinner.

cards.name: Art of Science 2026
cards.address: 475 Via Ortega, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Browse submissions celebrating the visual beauty of scientific research from campus departments.
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: Stevens Canyon Trail
cards.address: 13492 Stevens Canyon Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Stevens Canyon Trail with a crisp early walk before the crowds show up — the canyon stays shaded and cool in the morning, which is exactly what 55°F calls for.
cards.why: Cool weather and a quiet trail is the best possible Friday morning combo.

cards.name: Shoreline Amphitheatre
cards.address: Shoreline Amphitheatre
cards.blurb: Low-key arts stop.
cards.why: World-class outdoor concerts with a Bay backdrop — the big-ticket summer destination

cards.name: Cooper-Garrod Vineyards at Garrod Farms
cards.address: 22645 Garrod Rd, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Head up to Cooper-Garrod Vineyards at Garrod Farms in Saratoga, pour yourself a glass of estate wine, and sit outside with the vineyard and hills stretched out in front of you.
cards.why: 69°F and a hillside vineyard patio is a pretty easy sell on a Friday afternoon.

cards.name: Coterie Winery
cards.address: 885 W Julian St, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Solid local pick for a meal.
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Lace up and take the upper loop for the big panoramic payoff — the morning light on the Bay from up top is worth every bit of the climb.
cards.why: Cool temps and clear skies make this the ideal window to knock out the hike before the day gets going.

cards.name: Books Inc.
cards.address: Town and Country Village, 855 El Camino Real #74, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Browse the shelves at Books Inc. in Town and Country — it's a genuinely good indie shop with solid staff picks and plenty of room to wander without feeling rushed.
cards.why: A leisurely post-hike browse through a real bookstore beats any chain, and this one has personality.

cards.name: 1,000 Ways to Hold: A New Work By Erika Chong Shuch
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: See an interactive clay and conversation installation exploring ways of holding and connection.
cards.why: It's free, it's genuinely interesting, and it's the kind of thing you'll actually talk about later.

cards.name: Mian Sichuan Noodles (Cupertino) 滋味小麵
cards.address: 19389 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a bowl at Mian Sichuan Noodles on Stevens Creek — the noodles are hand-pulled and the broths run deep and spicy.
cards.why: This place punches well above its price point and lunch service wraps at 3, so the timing is perfect.
cards.venue: Mian Sichuan Noodles (Cupertino) 滋味小麵

cards.name: Halo pottery By Hila Itzhak | Pottery studio
cards.address: 632 Fremont Ave, Los Altos, CA 94024, USA
cards.blurb: Book a pottery session at Halo — Hila's studio is a proper hands-on experience where you actually make something, not just watch.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun afternoon activity for adults and a nice change of pace from another walk or museum.
cards.venue: Halo pottery By Hila Itzhak | Pottery studio

cards.name: Birk's
cards.address: 3955 Freedom Cir, Santa Clara, CA 95054, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Birk's for a proper steakhouse dinner — this is a sit-down, nice-booth, serious-food kind of spot where you order the steak and take your time.
cards.why: After a full day, a grown-up dinner at a place like this is exactly the right call.

cards.name: Santa Clara University
cards.address: 500 El Camino Real, Santa Clara, CA 95053, USA
cards.blurb: Take a slow Sunday morning walk through the Santa Clara University campus. The Spanish Colonial architecture looks great in the cool morning light, and the grounds are peaceful before the day gets going.
cards.why: It's a genuinely pretty campus to wander with coffee in hand.

cards.name: Rio Adobe Southwest Cafe
cards.address: 10525 S De Anza Blvd #100, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Rio Adobe is a casual Southwest Mexican spot with solid lunch plates. Order tacos or an enchilada combo and take your time — the portions are generous and the vibe is laid-back.
cards.why: Affordable, satisfying, and a good pick after a morning of walking and wine.

cards.name: Stevens Creek Trail
cards.address: Unnamed Road, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Stevens Creek Trail is flat and easygoing, great for a post-lunch stroll along the water. Walk as far as you feel like and double back whenever.
cards.why: The 67-degree afternoon weather is basically made for a casual trail walk.

cards.name: Elements Massage
cards.address: 308 W El Camino Real, Sunnyvale, CA 94087, USA
