cards.blurb: Elements Massage in Sunnyvale is a straightforward, no-fuss spot for a solid table massage. Book a 50-minute session and let the afternoon fully decompress.
cards.why: Ending an active Sunday with a massage is the right move.

cards.name: Kovai Cafe - Milpitas
cards.address: 181 Ranch Dr, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Kovai Cafe is a well-loved South Indian vegetarian spot with a full dinner menu. Go for the dosas or a thali if they have one — the food is fresh and the flavors are the real deal.
cards.why: A satisfying, lighter dinner that won't weigh you down at the end of a full day.

cards.name: Bill's Cafe
cards.address: Bill's Cafe, Willow Glen
cards.blurb: Start the morning at this Willow Glen breakfast staple. The eggs benedict and breakfast burritos are crowd favorites, and the vibe is exactly the kind of easygoing Saturday morning spot a family needs.
cards.why: Hard to beat a proper sit-down breakfast before a full day with kids.

cards.name: Chromatic Coffee
cards.address: Chromatic Coffee, San Jose
cards.blurb: Settle in with a well-crafted espresso drink or pour-over while the morning warms up — a solid spot to recharge between breakfast and lunch.
cards.why: A low-key coffee stop keeps the morning moving without rushing into the afternoon.

cards.name: Back A Yard Caribbean Grill
cards.address: Back A Yard, San Jose
cards.blurb: Swing into Back A Yard for lunch. The jerk chicken plate is the move, and the portions are generous enough that everyone leaves happy.
cards.why: One of the most loved lunch spots in downtown San Jose and totally family-friendly.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Spend the afternoon at the Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum. Real mummies, real hieroglyphics, and artifacts that genuinely impress kids and adults alike. Let the kids lead the way through the tomb replica.
cards.why: It's one of the most surprising gems in all of San Jose and kids absolutely love the mummies.

cards.name: San Jose Municipal Rose Garden
cards.address: Municipal Rose Garden
cards.blurb: Walk off the afternoon at the Municipal Rose Garden. May is peak bloom season, so the whole park is loaded with color. Great spot to let the kids run around while the grown-ups decompress.
cards.why: Free, beautiful, and the roses in early May are as good as it gets all year.

cards.name: The Garden Spa
cards.address: 5406 Thornwood Dr #130, San Jose, CA 95123, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the evening with a relaxing treatment — massages, facials, and body services available to shake off the day's steps.
cards.why: After a full afternoon outdoors, a little spa time is a natural way to close out the day.

cards.name: Manley’s Donuts
cards.address: 10991 N De Anza Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with a fresh donut (or three) from Manley's. Classic, no-frills, exactly what you want before the day gets going.
cards.why: Hard to beat a great donut spot on a cool Monday morning.

cards.name: Rancho San Antonio Open Space
cards.address: Rancho San Antonio County Park
cards.blurb: Hit the trails while the morning is still crisp. The upper Wildcat Loop gives you oak-lined ridgeline views without being brutal, and the farm along the lower trail is a nice detour on the way back.
cards.why: 55 degrees and sunny is the best possible weather for this hike.

cards.name: Los Altos Library
cards.address: 13 S San Antonio Rd, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Clean up and browse the Los Altos Library. Pick up something for the week, use a reading nook to decompress after the hike, or just wander the stacks.
cards.why: A genuinely nice library that's worth spending time in, not just passing through.

cards.name: The Good Salad
cards.address: 397 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Lunch at The Good Salad on Campbell Ave. Fresh, solid options that don't leave you dragging through the afternoon.
cards.why: It's the kind of lunch spot you actually feel good about after.

cards.name: Mio Vicino
cards.address: 1290 Benton St, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Mio Vicino, a neighborhood Italian spot in Santa Clara. Go for the pasta and don't skip the bread.
cards.why: Cozy Italian on a Monday night is always a good call.

cards.name: Monday Meditation & Mindfulness
cards.blurb: Practice guided meditation for 20 minutes, then ask questions with instructor Manisha.
cards.why: A genuinely nice way to close out a full Monday without any effort.

cards.name: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.address: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.blurb: Wander the Japanese estate gardens in Saratoga while it's still cool and quiet. The bamboo groves and koi ponds hit differently on a weekday morning when the crowds haven't shown up yet.
cards.why: Crisp morning air and a peaceful garden is a great combo on a day like today.

cards.name: Ridge Vineyards
cards.address: 17100 Montebello Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Head up Montebello Road to Ridge Vineyards for a tasting on the outdoor terrace. The views over the valley are stunning and the pours are serious, so take your time.
cards.why: You're already in the hills — might as well make the most of it.

cards.name: Cinco de Mayo at Cobre & Clay
cards.address: 301 S. Market Street, San Jose
cards.blurb: Enjoy Cinco de Mayo celebrations with food, drinks, and entertainment downtown.
cards.why: It's Cinco de Mayo and this is the place to actually feel it tonight.

cards.name: Blackberry Farm Golf Course
cards.address: 22100 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Knock out a few holes at Blackberry Farm Golf Course before dinner. It's a casual, well-maintained par-3 course that doesn't take itself too seriously.
cards.why: A round of golf in the late afternoon light is a genuinely good way to end the outdoors portion of the day.

cards.name: Barnes & Noble
cards.address: 3600 Stevens Creek Blvd, San Jose, CA 95117, USA
cards.blurb: Browse the shelves at Barnes and Noble on Stevens Creek for an hour. Grab a stack of things you'd never normally pick up and see what sticks.
cards.why: Low-key, pressure-free, and a nice wind-down after a day of wine and walking.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll or jog along this creekside trail winding through Campbell, where shaded paths and open green stretches make for a peaceful morning outside.
cards.why: A relaxed outdoor walk is a great way to ease into the day before the arboretum visit.

cards.name: Montalvo Arts Center Arboretum
cards.address: Montalvo Arts Center
cards.blurb: Wander the free outdoor trails through the Montalvo estate — the loop past the villa and through the wooded hillside is the move. No crowds on a weekday morning.
cards.why: It's one of those places locals keep to themselves, and a cool morning makes the walk genuinely pleasant.

cards.name: Cooper-Garrod Vineyards at Garrod Farms
cards.address: 22645 Garrod Rd, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Cooper-Garrod is a working horse ranch and vineyard rolled into one — taste through their estate wines on the outdoor patio and soak in the Santa Cruz Mountains backdrop. The Chardonnay and Cab Franc are the ones to try.
cards.why: Saratoga wine country at its most unpretentious, and they open right at noon.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: The Triton is Santa Clara's free contemporary art museum and it punches way above its weight. Walk the sculpture garden first, then work through the main galleries at whatever pace you want.
cards.why: Free admission, real art, and a sculpture garden that's perfect in 72-degree weather.

cards.name: Books Inc.
cards.address: 1875 S Bascom Ave #600, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Browse a well-curated selection of fiction, nonfiction, and local picks at this beloved indie bookstore — a perfect place to find your next read before tonight's book club.
cards.why: Picking up a new book right before the Sci-Fi & Fantasy Book Club is a fun way to arrive inspired.

cards.name: Sci-Fi & Fantasy Book Club
cards.blurb: Discuss Light From Uncommon Stars by Ryka Aoki with fellow sci-fi and fantasy readers.
cards.why: A free Tuesday night book club in Saratoga is a solid low-key ending to the day.

cards.name: Jonathan Calm - To Wherever, Forever: Archives of Absence & Sites of Passage
cards.blurb: Explore two linked exhibitions examining absence, memory, and migration through art.
cards.why: It's free, it's on today, and it's a genuinely interesting counterpoint to a winery afternoon.

cards.name: Santana Row Farmers Market
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose
cards.blurb: Shop for local produce, artisan goods, and ready-to-eat food weekly.
cards.why: A lively midday food stop that wraps up the arts-focused morning on a relaxed, tasty note.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Follow the paved creekside trail through shady trees and open meadows — a nice stretch for walking or light jogging alongside the water.
cards.why: A gentle afternoon walk helps reset after a busy morning of galleries and market browsing.

cards.name: Gali Vineyards
cards.address: 21 W Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Settle in for a tasting flight of small-batch wines poured in a charming downtown setting with a warm, laid-back atmosphere.
cards.why: A mellow late-afternoon stop that lets you unwind with a glass before the evening begins.

cards.name: DishDash
cards.address: DishDash, Sunnyvale
cards.blurb: Start the morning at DishDash with some strong coffee and a bite before the day gets going.
cards.why: It's a Sunnyvale staple and a solid way to kick things off.

cards.name: Drop-in Tech Help
cards.blurb: Get one-on-one help learning your laptop, tablet, or phone during drop-in tech sessions.
cards.why: Open late and always a good time when you just want to keep the night going.

cards.name: Alum Rock Park
cards.address: 15350 Penitencia Creek Rd, San Jose, CA 95127, USA
cards.blurb: Good place to walk and clear your head.
cards.why: A hidden gem in the foothills — peaceful and free, great for families and solo explorers

cards.name: J. Lohr Vineyards & Wines
cards.address: 1000 Lenzen Ave, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Swing into J. Lohr Vineyards for a tasting flight and explore what's pouring in their urban tasting room. It's a different vibe from the hilltop spots, more relaxed and approachable.
cards.why: A solid second stop if you want to keep the afternoon loose and low-key.

cards.name: SJSU Student Union Bowling Center
cards.address: 211 S 9th St, San Jose, CA 95192, USA
cards.blurb: Roll a few frames at this laid-back campus bowling alley — grab lane shoes, pick your ball, and settle into a casual evening of strikes and spares.
cards.why: A fun, low-key wind-down after a full day of wine tasting that keeps you in San Jose without backtracking.

cards.name: Chinese Medicine for Chronic Pain
cards.blurb: Listen to Dr. Julia Lee explain natural approaches to chronic pain management using Chinese medicine principles.
cards.why: A genuinely interesting evening lecture that gives the day a thoughtful, curious close after all the activity.

cards.name: DishDash
cards.address: DishDash, Sunnyvale
cards.blurb: Start the morning at DishDash in Sunnyvale — grab some strong coffee and a light bite before the day kicks off.
cards.why: It's a Sunnyvale staple and a solid way to ease into the day.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Wander the Triton Museum of Art in Santa Clara — the sculpture garden is especially good on a cool morning, and the contemporary galleries inside are free and genuinely worth your time.
cards.why: Arts have been light this week and this one punches way above its weight for a free museum.

cards.name: Oren's Hummus
cards.address: 261 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Oren's Hummus on University Ave in Palo Alto. Get the hummus plate, add some pita, and don't skip the falafel.
cards.why: One of the best lunch values in the whole South Bay and it's a quick hop up 101.

cards.name: Creative Break
cards.address: 110 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030
cards.blurb: Make art and unwind during a casual creative session.
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: Scotty McCreery
cards.address: 14831 Pierce Road
cards.blurb: Hear country music from Scotty McCreery in an outdoor vineyard setting.
cards.why: A live show at that venue on a Thursday night is hard to beat as a closer.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a walk along the Bay Trail as the mist burns off the water. It's wide open out here, good air, and the views stretch all the way across to the East Bay hills.
cards.why: Cool crisp morning air and a flat easy walk is the perfect way to ease into a Friday.

cards.name: Cupertino Farmers Market
cards.address: 21275 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino
cards.blurb: Shop for local produce, artisan goods, and ready-to-eat food weekly.
cards.why: It's free, it's lively, and grabbing a market breakfast beats any drive-through.

cards.name: Savannah-Chanelle Vineyards
cards.address: 23600 Congress Springs Rd, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Savannah-Chanelle is just down the road and pours a great lineup of Pinot Noir and Chardonnay on their shaded patio. Settle in with a glass and let lunch come to you slowly.
cards.why: Two mountain wineries back to back is exactly the kind of low-key afternoon this weather calls for.

cards.name: The Spa at Four Seasons Hotel Silicon Valley
cards.address: 2050 University Ave, East Palo Alto, CA 94303, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the afternoon at The Spa at Four Seasons Silicon Valley with a treatment or just use the facilities to decompress after the wine trail. It's a proper luxury spa, so budget accordingly.
cards.why: After a morning of walking and an afternoon of wine tasting, an hour of spa time hits different.
cards.venue: The Spa at Four Seasons Hotel Silicon Valley

cards.name: Coterie Winery
cards.address: 885 W Julian St, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Coterie Winery in San Jose opens at 5 and pours small-production wines in an intimate tasting room setting. This is a great spot to ease into the evening with something special.
cards.why: Their hours are perfectly timed for a Friday evening wind-down before dinner.

cards.name: Shoreline Amphitheatre
cards.address: Shoreline Amphitheatre
cards.blurb: Low-key arts stop.
cards.why: World-class outdoor concerts with a Bay backdrop — the big-ticket summer destination

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a walk along the bay trail while the fog burns off. It's quiet this early, the water is glassy, and you'll have the path mostly to yourselves.
cards.why: Cool crisp morning, no crowds, and it wakes you up better than coffee.

cards.name: Cupertino Farmers Market
cards.address: 21275 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino
cards.blurb: Shop for local produce, artisan goods, and ready-to-eat food weekly.
cards.why: Free, easy, and a genuinely nice way to ease into a Friday morning.

cards.name: Main Street Cupertino
cards.address: 19401 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Swing into Main Street Cupertino for lunch at one of the Asian restaurants along the strip or grab a table at Lazy Dog. Walk the outdoor plaza after to shake off the wine.
cards.why: Good food options, outdoor walkability, and it keeps you in the same pocket of the South Bay.

cards.name: Picchetti Winery
cards.address: 13100 Montebello Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the afternoon with a tasting flight at this hillside winery tucked into the Santa Cruz foothills above Cupertino — the views and pours pair nicely after a morning of shopping.
cards.why: You're already in Cupertino and this is a mellow, scenic way to bridge the gap before dinner.

cards.name: Hiroshi
cards.address: 328 Main St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Hiroshi on Main Street Los Altos is the kind of Japanese restaurant you make a reservation for. The omakase-style dishes are precise and the sake list is worth exploring.
cards.why: Friday night deserves a proper dinner, and this place delivers.

cards.name: Stevens Creek Trail
cards.address: Unnamed Road, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning on the Stevens Creek Trail — flat, easy, and genuinely pretty first thing when the light is low and the path isn't crowded yet.
cards.why: Cool crisp morning air and a mellow walk is the perfect way to ease into a Sunday.

cards.name: Mountain View Farmers Market
cards.address: 600 W Evelyn Ave, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Shop for local produce, artisan goods, and ready-to-eat food weekly.
cards.why: It's a farmers market Sunday and the timing lines up perfectly after your walk.

cards.name: Heritage Pub and Chop House
cards.address: 15 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Heritage Pub and Chop House in Los Gatos is the move for lunch — solid pub food, a good beer list, and a relaxed dining room that doesn't rush you out.
cards.why: A proper sit-down lunch after a morning outside hits different.

cards.name: Academy of Classical Ballet: The Four Seasons and Peter and the Wolf
cards.address: Heritage Theatre, 1 W. Campbell Ave #C32, Campbell, CA 95008
cards.blurb: Watch dancers perform two one-act ballets including Vivaldi's Four Seasons and Peter and the Wolf.
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: Perrucci Family Vineyard
cards.address: 15050 Kennedy Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Solid local pick for a meal.
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: 1 Oz Coffee
cards.address: 3051 Tasman Dr, Santa Clara, CA 95054, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a solid cup at 1 Oz Coffee before the day gets going.
cards.why: Good coffee at a reasonable hour sets the right tone.

cards.name: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.address: Hakone Estate & Gardens
cards.blurb: Walk the tiered Japanese gardens at Hakone Estate and take your time with the koi ponds, bamboo groves, and hillside views. It's genuinely peaceful on a weekday morning.
cards.why: The cool morning air makes this the best possible window to be here.

cards.name: Mount Eden Vineyards
cards.address: 22020 Mt Eden Rd, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Mount Eden Vineyards for a tasting before they close up for the afternoon. Small production, estate wines, and a ridgeline setting you won't get at most valley wineries.
cards.why: They're only open until 2 PM so hitting it late morning is the move.

cards.name: Bookasaurus
cards.address: 125 S Murphy Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Browse Bookasaurus just down the street on Murphy Ave. It's a used and new bookshop with good curation and the kind of shelves you can actually get lost in.
cards.why: Post-lunch browsing at an indie bookstore is exactly the kind of low-key afternoon win this day needs.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Stretch your legs along this tree-lined creekside trail winding through Campbell — great for a relaxed afternoon walk after a day of eating and browsing.
cards.why: It's a natural wind-down between Bookasaurus and dinner that keeps you right in the Campbell neighborhood.

cards.name: World Wrapps
cards.address: 1875 S Bascom Ave #165, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Solid local pick for a meal.
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: THE MARKET AT EDGEWOOD
cards.address: 2170 W Bayshore Rd, Palo Alto, CA 94303, USA
cards.blurb: Pick up something fresh for breakfast while the morning crowd is thin — they open at 6 AM so you'll beat the rush.
cards.why: Good way to ease into a Friday without committing to a full sit-down spot this early.

cards.name: Cupertino Farmers Market
cards.address: 21275 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino
cards.blurb: Shop for local produce, artisan goods, and ready-to-eat food weekly.
cards.why: It wraps at 1 PM so hitting it late morning gives you the full selection before it gets picked over.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Browse the contemporary exhibitions inside and then wander the sculpture garden out back at your own pace.
cards.why: It's free admission and genuinely impressive for a regional museum.

cards.name: Stanford Cardinal Baseball vs. North Carolina State Wolfpack Baseball
cards.address: 151 Sam McDonald Mall
cards.blurb: Watch Stanford baseball face North Carolina State in a college game.
cards.why: A fun outdoor sports fix to close out the day after a full afternoon of culture.

cards.name: $9 Before 9 pm Happy Hour
cards.address: 48 South 1st Street, San Jose
cards.blurb: Grab discounted drinks and appetizers before 9 pm at Nomikai.
cards.why: Great value evening drinks that cap off a busy Friday without any extra effort.

cards.name: Coterie Winery
cards.address: 885 W Julian St, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Drop into Coterie Winery for a glass of something small-batch and well-crafted in a laid-back tasting room setting — ideal for a relaxed Friday evening.
cards.why: A polished but unpretentious spot to transition from a full day into a mellow night.

cards.name: Evergreen Coffee Co
cards.address: 4075 Evergreen Village Square #150, San Jose, CA 95135, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Evergreen Coffee Co with a good cup and a chill vibe before the day kicks off.
cards.why: It's a solid local spot and a better start than a chain.

cards.name: Stevens Creek Trail
cards.address: Unnamed Road, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Take a relaxed walk along Stevens Creek Trail, flat and scenic with plenty of room to stretch out and move at whatever pace you want.
cards.why: Perfect morning weather for an easy trail walk.

cards.name: Black Mountain Trail
cards.address: Cristo Rey Dr, Los Altos, CA 94024, USA
cards.blurb: Stretch your legs on this scenic trail winding through the hills above Los Altos — great views and a peaceful mid-morning hike.
cards.why: A breezy outdoor reset between your morning trail run and the afternoon shopping stop.

cards.name: Heroes & Champions
cards.address: 574 E El Camino Real, Sunnyvale, CA 94087, USA
cards.blurb: Browse Heroes and Champions in Sunnyvale for comics, graphic novels, and collectibles — the kind of store you end up spending way more time in than planned.
cards.why: It's a genuinely well-stocked shop and a fun afternoon wander.

cards.name: Blue Danube Spa
cards.address: 200 California Ave suite 170, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Treat yourself to a relaxing spa session with massages and body treatments just in time for a proper Mother's Day wind-down.
cards.why: A calm, restorative stop that bridges the gap between your afternoon shopping and evening dinner in Palo Alto.

cards.name: Cafe Pro Bono
cards.address: 2437 Birch St, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Cafe Pro Bono in Palo Alto for a proper Italian dinner to close out the night, think pasta, good bread, and a glass of wine at a comfortable pace.
cards.why: It's a relaxed, well-loved spot to end a full day on a good note.
